metadata.procedure_phase: Stage 2 IN-PROGRESS at item 2.4. Stage 2 EA instance 3 has APPROVED comprehension gate (iteration 2, Guide-#4 verdict) and is starting work, but reported the work is too large for one session. EA will do a partial chunk of items 2.4 (and possibly some of 2.5-2.8) before emitting v3+ mid-stage handoff per i1-4 (docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor).
metadata.branch_at_handoff_blarai: chore/platform-extraction @ 3b9b1b8 (no new commits since v2 mid-stage handoff at instance 4 spawn)
metadata.branch_at_handoff_devplatform: main @ 3894221 (unchanged from instance 2 handoff; not re-verified this instance -- not in scope for Stage 2 mid-stage)
metadata.status_md_last_commit: aa09f6a (between-stages cleanup commit; UNCHANGED through instance 4)
metadata.fleet_pause_state: paused (per v2 mid-stage handoff fleet_paused_field; state.json NOT in current git status, so unchanged); successor should re-verify on first substantive analysis
metadata.worktree_state: working tree dirty with 8 expected Stage 2 mid-stage paths (4 modified + 4 untracked, matching v2 handoff EXACTLY); successor should re-verify with V0a-V0g style block from v2 mid-stage handoff
metadata.trigger_reason: LA-request triggered by EA reporting work is too large for one session. LA explicitly wants successor to have MAXIMUM context window for independent multi-file verification of EA's work product. LA emphasized "mature not minimal" and "critical operation" -- thoroughness expected, depth over speed.

guide_handoff_brief.active_conversational_topic:
Stage 2 EA instance 3's iteration-2 comprehension gate was APPROVED by Guide instance 4 (file at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml). EA acknowledged its 3 small acknowledgments (critical_notify.ps1 inspection rule + scope-expansion-stop-and-report rule + intent-to-proceed). EA began item 2.4 work (12 Python entrypoints argparse refactor) and reported the full Stage 2 remaining scope (items 2.4-2.10) is too large for one session. EA will do a partial chunk -- exact scope unknown but likely some subset of item 2.4, possibly extending into 2.5 -- and emit a v3+ mid-stage handoff per i1-4.
Successor's first substantive work will be analyzing EA's v3+ mid-stage handoff when it arrives. LA explicitly requested "independent analysis... ability to verify the entry points across multiple files" -- this is the multi-file disk-level verification of refactored Python entrypoints (and possibly PS1 scripts, MCP server) that the LA wants the successor to perform WITHOUT trusting the EA's "I did X" statements at face value. See w12 for the verification protocol.

decision.title: Iteration-1 Guide review attachment list refinement (10 files vs LA's proposed 11)
decision.what: LA proposed 11 attachments for the new EA instance 3 init message. I recommended 10: kept 9 of LA's, dropped VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md and RECOVERY.md (LA-scope, not EA-scope), added EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml back (LA had missed it; contains V1-V7 verification block verbatim).
decision.rationale: Original Stage 1->2 handoff explicitly excluded VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md and RECOVERY.md from EA attachments (LA cheat-sheet / panic-card scope). EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml is the source-of-truth for V1-V7 closure verification commands the EA needs at Stage 2 close.
decision.successor_instruction: If EA mid-stage handoff includes a "next attachments needed" list, validate it against this same 10-file pattern. EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml stays in the attachment chain through Stage 2 close.

decision.title: critical_notify.ps1 disposition (raised by EA in iteration-2 open question)
decision.what: EA's expanded 3-directory PS1 audit scope surfaced tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 (NOT in canonical 7). EA asked: add to canonical (becoming 8) or surface as defer-decision? My answer: NOT added to canonical. Per WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1, critical_notify.ps1's BlarAI reference is in a comment-example (line 11), not runtime code; intentionally excluded from 2.4.v2 additional_tools and G-1/G-4. Default: NO ACTION with documented Appendix A.1 citation. EA must inspect at item 2.5; if comment-only confirmed, document and skip; if runtime path discovered, STOP and report to LA (no silent scope expansion).
decision.rationale: Stage 2 work is already large; silent scope expansion mid-stage is a common failure mode for HIGH-risk stages. The Appendix A.1 audit was thorough; trust it as default while requiring physical inspection as belt-and-suspenders. The "NEVER silent scope expansion" rule generalizes to any other PS1 the audit surfaces.
decision.successor_instruction: If EA mid-stage handoff says "added critical_notify.ps1 to scope" without citing a discovered runtime path, push back -- that violates the inspection rule. Default = no-action; expansion only on explicit LA approval after a discovered runtime path. Same rule for any other PS1 from the expanded audit scope.

decision.title: Iteration-1 Guide review file format (per p13 + this session)
decision.what: LA explicitly granted file-write permission to write the iteration-2 Guide review to a file in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/, with brief chat pointer. Reused the same pattern for iteration-1 review.
decision.rationale: Long structured outputs the LA forwards to another agent benefit from file output (per p13 / feedback_long_outputs_to_file). The "temp_for_responses" folder is the LA's established holding location.
decision.successor_instruction: Continue this pattern for all long Guide responses (iteration reviews, mid-stage analysis reports). File path: docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_*. Brief chat summary + file pointer.

decision.title: Iteration-2 Guide review verdict APPROVED with one substantive answer
decision.what: EA's iteration-2 comprehension gate addressed all 8 corrections from iteration 1. Pickup verification re-confirmed PASS. 12 Python + 7 PS1 explicit enumeration. -BlarAIRoot standardization. C4 (vikunja_mcp/server.py) + C6 (extended grep patterns) committed. Risk labels descriptive (compound labels with reference prefixes acceptable per Guide-#4 ruling). Rollback acknowledged. w3 preflight committed. Verdict: APPROVED with answer to critical_notify.ps1 open question + 3 small acknowledgments required (no further re-review needed for those acks; EA proceeds after emitting them).
decision.rationale: Iteration-2 quality was high; 8 corrections addressed substantively. The compound-label ruling (e.g., "R12-PS1-coverage-watch" is acceptable when it's a derived label with anchor field disambiguating from the source ID R12) was a Guide-#4 micro-decision letting EA proceed without further label-bikeshedding.
decision.successor_instruction: If EA's mid-stage handoff or final closing handoff uses risk labels like "R12-X" or "G-1-Y" with hyphenated descriptive suffixes, accept them. Bare "V1" / "R12" / "C3" without descriptive suffix is the violation pattern (collides with source IDs).

decision.title: The 'Oopsy' was benign (no actual procedural problem)
decision.what: Session was named "Guide 2.4 Oopsy". I asked LA in initial brief what specifically went wrong. LA clarified: just shut down computer mid-Guide-instance-3 session; no procedural failure. Instance 3 had reviewed iteration-1 of EA-instance-2's gate (the v2 mid-stage handoff EA), did not emit its own handoff, and was simply terminated by power-off. Instance 4 (me) is the next active Guide; LA spawned me before spawning EA instance 3.
decision.successor_instruction: If LA references "instance 3" as a Guide that did substantive work, scope it to: one iteration of comprehension-gate review for the v2 mid-stage handoff EA (commit 3b9b1b8); no handoff brief emitted; no other artifacts.

decision.title: Risk-label hygiene compound-form ruling (Guide-#4 micro-decision)
decision.what: EA-instance-3 used compound labels in iteration 2 like "R12-PS1-coverage-watch" and "w3-blarai-pid-preflight-watch". Strictly speaking, the R12 prefix overlaps with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW §0.6 R12. I ruled these acceptable because the anchor field disambiguates from the source ID -- the label is "R12-PS1-coverage-watch" (a unique compound name), not "R12" (which would be a relabeling).
decision.rationale: Forcing pure-descriptive labels was bikeshedding for an iteration-2 acceptance that already fixed all 8 substantive corrections. Compound-with-anchor is sufficiently distinguishable.
decision.successor_instruction: Accept compound labels with reference prefixes when the anchor field is present and the suffix is descriptive. REJECT bare numeric labels without descriptive suffix.

decision.title: One-time file-write authorizations for Guide (this instance)
decision.what: LA granted file-write permission three times in this session: (1) iteration-2 Guide review XML, (2) iteration-1 Guide review XML (LA saved manually then), (3) this handoff brief itself (current LA instruction "prepare what I need to start a new Guide Session"). Each grant was scoped to docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/.
decision.rationale: Same as predecessor d8 pattern. Per dn12, do NOT assume permission carries forward.
decision.successor_instruction: Default to advisory-only. Per-request file-write grants only. The "temp_for_responses" folder is the established holding location for chat-paste artifacts that flow between agents.

open_watchpoints.intro: All 11 watchpoints from instance 2's handoff (w1 through w11) carry forward unchanged. Four new watchpoints introduced this instance (w12 through w15) follow.

watchpoint.description: Agents Cadence Monitor LogonType=S4U; Stage 4.7 Unregister + Register requires elevated PowerShell.
G-2 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory rewrite.

watchpoint.recheck_at: Stage 2.8 (FIRST INVOCATION; EA-instance-3 has acknowledged stop-and-report rule per Guide-#4 correction #8)
watchpoint.description: $BLARAI_PID preflight at item 2.8. EA committed to STOP and report if regex returns empty or non-3.

watchpoint.status_change: C4 + C6 now in EA scope per Guide-#4 correction #5; verify EA delivers in mid-stage handoff. C2 still unscoped.
watchpoint.description: Deferred items: C2 (full _vikunja_client.py impl beyond D.1 gate), C4 (MCP server entrypoint audit -- NOW EA-COMMITTED), C6 (broader Vikunja-client grep -- NOW EA-COMMITTED).
S-1 / S-3 / A3-followup -> DevPlatform-Meta tickets at Stage 6.
Stale wake_launcher auto-stash at stash@{0}. DO NOT POP through any platform-separation stage.
Optional Vikunja project rename ("BlarAI Agent Gates" -> "Agent Gates").

watchpoint.recheck_at: EA mid-stage handoff arrival (likely sometime in this successor's session)
watchpoint.description: **Multi-file entrypoint verification.** LA explicitly requested independent verification of refactored entrypoints across multiple files. When EA's mid-stage handoff arrives, the successor MUST execute the following verification protocol: (a) `git status --porcelain` and inspect ALL modified Python files (expect at least some subset of the 12 Python entrypoints touched). (b) For EACH modified Python entrypoint: read the file (or grep) and verify both `parser.add_argument('--project-root', ...)` AND `parser.add_argument('--project-id', type=int, ...)` lines exist. (c) For EACH modified entrypoint: verify it actually CALLS `_project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id)` (or equivalent) -- the argparse declaration is necessary but NOT sufficient; the resolve() call is what makes the seam functional. (d) For each modified PS1: verify `[string]$BlarAIRoot` parameter with the env-fallback default `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })`. For the 4 G-1 scripts, verify ZERO remaining `$RepoRoot` references via grep. (e) For tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py if modified: verify list_tasks / search_tasks handlers route through `_vikunja_client.py` import, not direct HTTP/REST/httpx/aiohttp calls. (f) Cross-check the EA's reported "files touched" count against actual `git status --porcelain` to detect silent omissions. (g) Run `pytest tools/ --tb=short -q` and confirm baseline holds at >= 280 passed (regression detection per s2w8 from instance 2).
watchpoint.source: LA direct request 2026-04-25 ("independent analysis will need to be able to verify the entry points across multiple files")

watchpoint.description: **Single-commit discipline + handoff XML emission.** EA reported work too large for one session => MUST emit v3+ mid-stage handoff per i1-4. The handoff XML is a docs-only commit -- ZERO refactor work in that commit. Successor MUST verify on EA report: (a) `git log --oneline -3` shows ONE new commit (the v3+ mid-stage handoff XML, docs-only). (b) `git status --porcelain` shows the in-progress refactor work UNCOMMITTED on disk (matching the same 8-paths + new modifications pattern from the v2 mid-stage handoff -- the count grows but the discipline holds). (c) NO partial-commit attempts that fold refactor work into the handoff commit. (d) NO "emergency single-commit" that omits items not yet done. If ANY of (a)-(d) is violated, this is a HARD FAIL -- EA misunderstood i1-4. Successor reviews as REJECTED, instructs EA to restart from clean state.
watchpoint.source: i1-4 acknowledgment in v2 mid-stage handoff; EA-instance-3 ack g4-7 + ack i1-4 verbatim

watchpoint.description: **critical_notify.ps1 inspection result.** EA must inspect tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 at item 2.5 and report disposition: (a) confirmed comment-only per Appendix A.1 = no-action documented; (b) runtime path discovered = STOP and report (no silent scope expansion). Successor MUST check the EA's mid-stage handoff for this disposition and validate it matches Appendix A.1 inspection (or explicit LA approval if scope expanded).
watchpoint.source: Guide-#4 iteration-2 answer_to_ea_open_question + EA-instance-3 ack g4-1 (critical_notify inspection rule)

watchpoint.description: **Legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py dual-touch consistency.** This legacy file is BOTH a load_config caller (refactored at item 2.4) AND a candidate for fold-or-route decision (at item 2.6). EA committed to consistent treatment; successor MUST verify the EA's disposition decision in mid-stage handoff (which option chosen: route through new tools/_vikunja_client.py chokepoint OR fold in entirely) and that the disposition is consistent across both items 2.4 and 2.6. Inconsistent treatment = silent semantic drift hazard for any code that imports `from tools.fleet_observability._vikunja_client import ...` post-cutover.
watchpoint.source: v2 mid-stage handoff midflight observation v2-2 + Guide-#4 iteration-1 correction #4

guide_handoff_brief.anomalies_not_yet_in_status: None. STATUS.md last touched at aa09f6a (between-stages cleanup commit, instance 2). All decisions and risk-tracking from instance 4 live in this handoff brief + the three Temp-for-responses files; promotion to STATUS happens at item 2.10 close (which the EA-instance-3 will do, or a later EA instance if more handoffs are needed).

la_profile.intro: All 13 traits from instance 2's la_profile (p1-p13) carry forward and were re-confirmed during instance 4 work. Three new observations follow as p14-p16.
la_profile.trait: Non-dev "vibe coder". Re-confirmed throughout instance 4 -- LA relies on Guide for path-correction rulings, parameter-name standardization, and verification-strategy design.
Prefers XML-formatted Guide-to-EA directives. Re-confirmed -- iteration-1 init message (XML), iteration-1 + iteration-2 Guide responses (XML).

**"Mature not minimal" motto.** STRONGLY re-confirmed at handoff trigger -- LA explicitly said "The motto is mature not minimal" when emphasizing this is a critical operation and the successor needs maximum context window.
Tables faster than prose. Maintained throughout instance 4.
Forensic analysis. Implicit in LA's request for independent multi-file verification of EA work product.
Paste-attachment hazards. Not exercised this instance.
IDE focus reminders are context only. Not exercised.
One numbered list per response block. Maintained.
Welcomes proactive handoff recommendations at boundaries. LA initiated this handoff explicitly when EA reported work too large.
Spell out acronyms on first use. Maintained throughout instance 4 (EA, LA, MCP, REST, PS1, ADR, MCP, etc.).
Guided expertise vs menu-of-options. Re-confirmed in attachment-list refinement (LA proposed 11; I gave a recommendation, not a vote) and in critical_notify.ps1 disposition.
Per-request file-write grants only. Re-confirmed three times in this session.
Two-file pattern (XML directive + Markdown brief). Single-file pattern was sufficient this session (just XML for Guide reviews). Reuse two-file pattern for any longer Guide-to-EA directive.

**"temp_for_responses" folder convention.** docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ is the LA's established holding location for chat-paste artifacts that flow between agents. Both Guide responses and EA responses go there with `2.4-` prefix (procedure-stage indicator). Successor should write all long Guide-output artifacts to this folder unless LA specifies otherwise. Despite the "Temp" name, files there are audit artifacts -- DO NOT delete (per dn13).

**LA verifies XML output meticulously.** Caught a "TT22:48Z" double-T typo in iteration-1 Guide review timestamp (artifact of HH:MM placeholder substitution). Successor MUST emit complete timestamps without placeholders, OR explicitly flag placeholders so LA can substitute cleanly. Use full ISO 8601 format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

**"Critical operation" + "mature not minimal" = thoroughness signal.** When the LA flags a "critical operation" or invokes the "mature not minimal" motto, treat it as an explicit signal for thoroughness -- NOT speed. Default to deeper independent verification (read source files, grep on disk, run actual commands rather than trusting reports), longer documentation, more explicit acknowledgment chains. The LA prefers thorough analysis over quick approval; do NOT shortcut analysis to save context window.

active_risk_ids.intro: Risk IDs that proved active during instance 4. Some are carryforward; some are status-changed; some are new.
active_risk_ids.risk: Worktree contamination. Re-checked clean at every prior gate.
S4U elevation; CRITICAL for Stage 4.7. See w1.
resume_fleet vs unpause_fleet. EA-instance-3 verbatim ack n=2 commits to never call resume_fleet during Stage 2.
Stage 4.7 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory. Watchpoint w2.
$BLARAI_PID resolution. EA-instance-3 ack g4-5 verbatim; first invocation imminent at item 2.8 if EA reaches it before mid-stage handoff. See w3.
VIKUNJA_PASS literal. Full closure at post-soak.
Multi-BlarAI-projects discovery. A8/A9 promoted at instance 2 cleanup; A10 deferred to Stage 6.
REST vs MCP for Vikunja access. vikunja_access_protocol block in Stages 2/3/4.
_project_context.py schema mismatch. See w3 for first-invocation watch.

tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py inclusion in item 2.4 audit. EA committed via iteration-2 ack g4-4. Successor verifies in EA mid-stage handoff that server.py was actually touched and routes through _vikunja_client.

Extended grep patterns at item 2.6 (httpx, aiohttp, urllib, 127.0.0.1, env-var URLs, f-strings, websockets). EA committed via ack g4-4. Successor verifies grep results delivered in EA mid-stage handoff (or final handoff if 2.6 not reached this chunk).
Stage 2 rollback explicitly acknowledged by EA in part_f_rollback_acknowledgment block. Verbatim restatement of Guide-#4 correction-#7 required_action text.
LA explicitly requested multi-file disk-level verification of entrypoint refactor work. See w12.
EA reported work too large -> v3+ mid-stage handoff coming. Successor must verify handoff is docs-only + uncommitted refactor on disk pattern. See w13.
tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py is touched by BOTH item 2.4 (load_config caller) AND item 2.6 (chokepoint decision). Successor verifies consistent treatment. See w15.
critical_notify.ps1 default disposition = no-action; runtime-path discovery requires LA approval before scope expansion. See w14.

Compound labels with reference prefixes ("R12-X-watch") are acceptable when anchor field disambiguates from source ID. Bare "V1" / "R12" / "C3" without descriptive suffix is the violation pattern. EA-instance-3 follows this discipline correctly in iteration 2.

source_doc_revisions.intro: Files touched during instance 4 lifetime. STATUS.md, all stage XMLs, all source-of-truth docs UNCHANGED.

revision.commits: not committed (LA may commit after Stage 2 close as audit artifact, or leave in Temp folder)
revision.note: Iteration-1 Guide instance 4 review of EA-instance-3 comprehension gate. Verdict: APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS. 8 corrections (3 HIGH, 4 MED, 1 LOW). **Important historical context for the EA's iteration-2 gate; successor MUST read this file for pattern reference.**

revision.commits: not committed (chat-paste artifact saved by LA)
revision.note: EA-instance-3 iteration-2 comprehension gate (response to Guide-#4 corrections). All 8 corrections addressed. Pickup verification re-confirmed PASS. 12 Python + 7 PS1 enumeration explicit (with on-disk Get-ChildItem evidence). -BlarAIRoot standardization. C4/C6 committed. Open question on critical_notify.ps1 (answered in iteration-2 Guide review). **Successor MUST read this file -- it defines the EA's committed work plan and 23 binding acknowledgments.**

Iteration-2 Guide instance 4 review. Verdict: APPROVED. Three small acknowledgments required (critical_notify.ps1 inspection rule, scope-expansion-stop-and-report rule, intent-to-proceed). EA proceeds to item 2.4 work. **Successor MUST read this file -- it contains the answer_to_ea_open_question on critical_notify.ps1 and the standing reminders for the EA throughout Stage 2.**

revision.commits: not yet committed; LA action: save to docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml (overwriting predecessor instance-2 handoff) and commit BEFORE spawning successor Guide
revision.note: You are reading this. Predecessor (instance 2 -> 3) handoff at GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml is recoverable from git history (commit ef0e30e and parents).

source_doc_revisions.revision_no_change: No changes to STATUS.md, any 0X_STAGEN_*.xml, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md, WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md, RECOVERY.md, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE.md, VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md, 00_MASTER_PLAN.md, GUIDE_PROMPT.xml, GUIDE_USAGE.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, devplatform/projects/registry.yaml, BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml. The 8 Stage 2 working files (4 modified + 4 untracked from items 2.1-2.3) remain on disk uncommitted, awaiting fold into single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 (or a v3+ mid-stage handoff if EA emits one before reaching 2.9 -- which is the EXPECTED path now per LA's "work too large" report).

first_action_for_successor.step: After your Variant B comprehension gate is complete, briefly acknowledge the handoff and flag any STATUS drift or git advance since 2026-04-25T23:50:00Z. Compare branch_at_handoff_blarai (3b9b1b8), status_md_last_commit (aa09f6a), worktree_state (8 expected entries) against actual repo state. Expected new commits: only the LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml itself (this file) and possibly the EA's v3+ mid-stage handoff XML if EA emitted before successor was spawned.

**Read all three Temp-for-responses files in addition to the Variant B mandatory reads:** (a) `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml` (iteration-1 Guide review with 8 corrections) (b) `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml` (EA iteration-2 gate) (c) `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml` (iteration-2 Guide review, APPROVED) They give you the iteration history and the in-flight commitments the EA made.
Wait for the Lead Architect (LA) to paste the EA-instance-3's mid-stage handoff XML when it arrives.

**When the EA's mid-stage handoff arrives, execute INDEPENDENT MULTI-FILE VERIFICATION per w12 + w13 + w14 + w15.** Per dn14 + dn17 + p16, do NOT trust the EA's "I did X" reports without disk verification: (a) `git log --oneline -3` -- expect ONE new docs-only commit (the v3+ mid-stage handoff XML). (b) `git status --porcelain` -- expect uncommitted refactor work matching what the EA reports as "completed but uncommitted". (c) For EACH Python file the EA reports as touched: read it, grep for both argparse flags, grep for `_project_context.resolve()` call. Don't trust the count -- verify on disk. (d) For EACH PS1 file the EA reports as touched: read it, grep for `[string]$BlarAIRoot` parameter and the env-fallback default. For G-1 scripts, grep for ANY remaining `$RepoRoot` references (should be zero). (e) For tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py if reported touched: read it, verify list_tasks/search_tasks handlers route through `_vikunja_client.py` import. (f) If item 2.6 grep audit reported: verify EA used the extended C6 pattern set (httpx/aiohttp/urllib/127.0.0.1/env-var URLs/f-strings/websockets), not the narrow XML-only 3 patterns. (g) Check critical_notify.ps1 disposition matches inspection rule (no-action with Appendix A.1 citation OR runtime-path discovery + LA approval). (h) Run `pytest tools/ --tb=short -q` if EA reports tests run -- confirm baseline >= 280 passed. (i) Cross-reference the EA's "files touched" list against `git status --porcelain` to detect silent omissions.

Return a `<guide_review_response>` XML verdict for the EA's mid-stage handoff (APPROVED to continue OR APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS for partial corrections OR REJECTED if discipline violated). Use `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/` for the file output (per p14 / d3).

prime_v3_handoff_watchpoints.point: **Single-commit discipline (w13).** EA's mid-stage handoff MUST be a docs-only commit. If `git log --oneline -1` shows the new commit touched ANY file under tools/ or pyproject.toml, the EA violated i1-4. REJECT and require restart from clean state.
**Items completed reporting accuracy (w12).** Cross-check EA's "items 2.4 partial" claim against actual disk state. Don't trust the report; run grep / Test-Path / pytest yourself for every claim.

**v3+ schema fidelity.** EA's mid-stage handoff MUST follow v1/v2 schema (metadata, ground_truth_at_handoff, work_completed, uncommitted_on_disk, work_remaining, critical_carryovers, single_commit_discipline, successor_pickup_verification, do_not). Schema deviation hints at lower-quality handoff -- inspect carefully.

**V0a-V0g equivalent for next EA successor.** EA's v3+ handoff should include a fresh pickup-verification block reflecting the new on-disk state (more files modified than the v2 8-entry baseline). Verify the block exists, outputs are reproducible, and counts match disk reality.

**All 23 acknowledgments STILL binding.** Successor EA picking up from v3+ handoff inherits same 16 pre-existing + 7 Guide-#4 + (any new from this Guide instance 5 review). Verify acknowledgment chain isn't broken.
**If EA reached item 2.8 (preflight):** verify $BLARAI_PID == 3. If empty or non-3: REJECT and STOP per w3.
**If EA reached item 2.6 (Vikunja audit):** verify extended grep used. If only 3-pattern XML grep used: REQUIRE re-run with extended C6 patterns before proceeding.
**If EA reached item 2.7 (pytest):** verify baseline >= 280 passed. Regression = REJECT.
**Risk label hygiene.** Compound labels with reference prefixes OK; bare numeric labels NOT OK (per d6).
**If EA emits v4+ instead of v3:** verify EA didn't lose the v1/v2/v3 chain. Each version SUPPLEMENTS prior; none are deleted from disk per dn7-equivalent rule.

do_not_list.intro: Carry forward all 12 prohibitions from instance 2 (dn1-dn12). Add 5 new (dn13-dn17) for instance 4 work products + LA-emphasized "mature not minimal" mandate.
do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT re-chase STATUS.md line 11 v2-banner HEAD staleness. Known chicken-and-egg.
Pre-execution invariant past-tense issue is resolved.
Do NOT edit, or allow any Stage 1-5 EA to edit, BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md. V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred.
Do NOT confuse fleet pause with Vikunja server stop. Different concerns.

prohibition.status_change: ACTIVE -- Stage 2 in progress; HIGH risk

do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT relax acknowledgment-wait for Stage 2 or Stage 4 EA. Strict verbatim enforcement.
Do NOT debug a "physform-extraction" typo. Chat-only artifact in a prior EA summary; never landed in any file.
Do NOT regenerate or revise Stage 0 / Stage 1 baseline JSON / exit reports. Frozen audit artifacts.

prohibition.status_change: reapplied at Stage 2 boundary; this handoff is at mid-stage which is ACCEPTABLE per LA-request trigger

do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT emit a handoff after only a partial Stage UNLESS LA-requested.
Do NOT modify this handoff brief or any predecessor handoff brief in git history. Permanent records.
Do NOT allow any Stage 1-5 EA to run `git stash pop` / `git stash apply` on stash@{0}.
Do NOT delete, modify, or "improve" GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_DIRECTIVE.xml or GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_BRIEF.md. Audit artifacts.
Do NOT assume LA's file-write permission to Guide carries forward across instances or even across topics within an instance.

**Do NOT delete or "clean up" the four Temp-for-responses files** (iteration-1 Guide review, EA iteration-2 gate, iteration-2 Guide review, this handoff brief). They are committed audit artifacts (or will be at Stage 2 close). The "temp_for_responses" folder is the LA's established holding location -- it is NOT actually temporary in the disposable sense (per p14). They travel with the docs/platform_separation/ archive at Stage 6.9.

**Do NOT trust the EA's "I did X" reports without disk-level verification** when the LA emphasizes "critical operation" or "mature not minimal" (which they did at handoff trigger). Per w12, run grep / Test-Path / read-file / pytest yourself for every claim. The successor's value is independent verification, not narrative trust. Specifically: argparse flag declaration is necessary but not sufficient -- ALSO verify the resolve() call.

**Do NOT accept a partial-commit Stage-2 mid-stage handoff.** Per i1-4 + w13, the handoff XML is a docs-only commit. If EA's commit touches tools/ or pyproject.toml, REJECT and require restart from clean state. The discipline matters even more in v3+ because partial commits compound.

**Do NOT silently approve scope expansion** (e.g., adding critical_notify.ps1 to canonical 7) without the EA citing a discovered runtime BlarAI path AND LA approval. Default disposition for any audit-surfaced PS1 is no-action with Appendix A.1 citation. See w14.

**Do NOT shortcut analysis to save context window.** The LA explicitly chose this handoff so the successor would have FULL context for thorough verification. If you find yourself thinking "I'll just trust the EA on this one" -- that's a signal to verify on disk instead. Per p16 and the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate, depth wins over speed for this critical operation. The verification protocol in w12 + step 4 of first_action_for_successor is the floor, not the ceiling.
